title: Update pn ready query to include unplanned bugs
description: Change ready_issues() in db.rs to remove the hardcoded bug exclusion. Replace 'issue_type IN (task, test, chore)' with a condition that includes bugs only when they have zero non-closed fixes children: (issue_type \!= 'bug' OR id NOT IN (SELECT fixes FROM issues WHERE fixes IS NOT NULL AND status \!= 'closed')). See specs/pensa.md pn ready section. Source: crates/pensa/src/db.rs:560-606.
close_reason: Implemented: ready_issues now includes unplanned bugs (no fix tasks) and excludes planned bugs (with open/in-progress fix tasks). Updated 1 unit test, added 3 integration tests.

title: Update auto-close to all-or-nothing for multi-fix bugs
description: Change close_issue() in db.rs to check whether ALL issues with fixes pointing to the same bug are closed before auto-closing the bug. Currently (db.rs:312-327) the first fix task to close immediately closes the bug. New behavior: query 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE fixes = ?bug_id AND status \!= closed AND id \!= ?closing_task_id'. If count > 0, skip auto-close. If 0, auto-close with reason 'fixed' (not 'fixed by <task-id>' since multiple tasks may contribute). See specs/pensa.md fixes field description. Source: crates/pensa/src/db.rs:312-327.
close_reason: Implemented all-or-nothing auto-close for multi-fix bugs in close_issue(). Updated unit test and added integration test.

title: Update existing fixes_auto_close and ready_excludes_bugs tests
description: Update the two existing tests in crates/pensa/tests/integration.rs and crates/pensa/src/db.rs that test the old behavior: (1) fixes_auto_close (integration.rs:176-205, db.rs:1591-1634) — should still pass since single-fix auto-close is unchanged, but verify the auto-close reason is now 'fixed' instead of 'fixed by <task-id>'. (2) ready_excludes_bugs (integration.rs:364-383) — invert the assertion: bugs should now APPEAR in pn ready when unplanned. Source: crates/pensa/tests/integration.rs, crates/pensa/src/db.rs unit tests.
close_reason: Tests already updated in prior commits — no changes needed

Collect resolved paths for injection. See specs/ralph.md 'System Prompt Injection > Sources'.

Skip when --command is set (test mode). See specs/ralph.md 'System Prompt Injection > Claude Invocation'.

See specs/springfield.md 'Prompt Delivery'.

Add --log-file flag to ralph
description: Ralph should accept an optional --log-file path and tee its output there directly, instead of sgf capturing ralph's stdout and teeing it in orchestrate.rs. This removes the awkward layering where sgf spawns ralph, pipes stdout, and writes to a log file on ralph's behalf.

title: Separate SIGINT and SIGTERM signal handling
description: Replace shared AtomicBool for SIGINT+SIGTERM with: (1) AtomicUsize sigint_count for SIGINT via signal_hook::flag::register_usize, (2) AtomicBool interrupted for SIGTERM only. Update all non-AFK interrupt checks to use sigint_count >= 1 || interrupted. See specs/ralph.md § Signal Handling. Source: crates/ralph/src/main.rs lines 132-134 (signal registration), 185-213 (interrupt checks).

title: Implement double Ctrl+C confirmation in run_afk
description: In run_afk(), change the interrupt polling loop to implement double-press logic: (1) When sigint_count == 1: print 'Press Ctrl+C again to stop' to stdout, record Instant::now() as confirmation window start. (2) When sigint_count >= 2: kill child, return. (3) On each poll iteration, if confirmation window is active and 2 seconds have elapsed: reset sigint_count to 0 via store(0, Relaxed). Message goes to stdout only (not log file). See specs/ralph.md § Double Ctrl+C in AFK Mode. Source: crates/ralph/src/main.rs lines 343-471 (run_afk function).

title: Integration test: AFK double Ctrl+C abort
description: Add integration test that sends two SIGINTs to the ralph process in AFK mode and verifies: (1) exit code 130, (2) stdout contains 'Press Ctrl+C again to stop'. Use the existing mock script pattern (RALPH_COMMAND) with a long-running fixture. Send first SIGINT, verify process is still alive, send second SIGINT, verify exit code 130. See specs/ralph.md § Testing → Integration Tests table. Source: crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs.

title: Integration test: AFK single Ctrl+C resets after timeout
description: Add integration test that sends one SIGINT to the ralph process in AFK mode and verifies the loop continues after the 2-second timeout. Use a short mock that completes quickly (1 iteration). Send SIGINT, wait >2s, verify process completes normally (exit code 0 or 2, not 130). See specs/ralph.md § Testing → Integration Tests table. Source: crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs.

title: Make spec optional in Build/Test CLI commands (main.rs). Change spec: String to spec: Option<String> in both variants. Update run_loop calls to pass spec.as_deref(). See specs/springfield.md sections 2+5, crates/springfield/src/main.rs:23-25,:44-46

Update test prompt template to handle missing SGF_SPEC. Change line 1 of templates/test.md from hardcoded --spec $SGF_SPEC to conditionally include --spec only when SGF_SPEC is set. See crates/springfield/templates/test.md:1

Update existing tests in orchestrate.rs and prompt.rs for optional spec. Add test cases for build/test without spec (no --spec flag, no SGF_SPEC env). Verify existing spec-required tests still pass. See crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs:342-785, crates/springfield/src/prompt.rs:26-91
Update specs/springfield.md docs: verify all references to sgf build and sgf test use [spec] (optional) syntax. Check default config table row if present. See specs/springfield.md
close_reason: Updated specs/springfield.md: made SGF_SPEC optional in invocation line, clarified --spec flag is optional in CLI flags table, clarified SGF_SPEC is only set when spec is provided in env vars table

title: Integration test: run sgf build without a spec argument end-to-end. Verify it launches ralph without --spec flag and without SGF_SPEC env var. Verify sgf build <spec> still works with spec validation.
ralph test prompt_files_default_entries_passed_when_unset fails when ~/.MEMENTO.md exists on host
close_reason: fixed

title: fix: Isolate HOME in prompt_files_default_entries_passed_when_unset test to prevent false failure when ~/.MEMENTO.md exists on host. Set HOME env var to temp dir so default PROMPT_FILES resolution is deterministic.

sgf kills ralph on first Ctrl+C in AFK mode — double-press never works. orchestrate.rs registers SIGINT with AtomicBool (single-press semantics), so sgf sends SIGTERM to ralph within 50ms of the first Ctrl+C, before the user can press again. Ralph's double-Ctrl+C mechanism is correct but never gets a chance to execute.
close_reason: fixed

title: Replace AtomicBool SIGINT handler with AtomicUsize counter in orchestrate::run_ralph for AFK mode. Use signal_hook::low_level::register (like ralph does) to increment a counter. While the ralph child is running in AFK mode: first SIGINT does nothing (let ralph handle it), second SIGINT within 2s sends SIGTERM to ralph child + stops sandbox + exits 130, timeout resets counter to 0. SIGTERM always kills immediately. Outside the child-running loop (pre-launch, daemon startup, etc.), single SIGINT exits immediately.

Add LoopConfig.afk flag plumbing so orchestrate::run knows whether to use double-press or single-press SIGINT semantics. The afk field already exists on LoopConfig — verify run_ralph receives it and gates the double-press logic on it.

Add integration test for AFK double Ctrl+C in sgf orchestrate. Use a mock ralph script that sleeps for 10s. Send two SIGINTs to the sgf process within 1s. Assert exit code 130. Also test that a single SIGINT does NOT kill the process within the 2s window (mock ralph exits 0 after 3s — verify sgf exits 0, not 130).

Update springfield spec docs: the Exit Codes section already updated with double-Ctrl+C behavior for AFK loops. Review the updated text in specs/springfield.md for accuracy after implementation is complete.
Sandbox 'already exists' message leaks into ralph output
description: ralph's ensure_sandbox() calls `docker sandbox create` without checking if the sandbox already exists. When it does exist, Docker prints 'sandbox with name claude-<workspace> already exists' to stdout, which leaks into ralph's output between the banner and the first iteration. Fix: extract a sandbox_name() helper (shared with configure_sandbox_network), check `docker sandbox ls -q` for the name, and only call `create` if absent. Suppress stdout/stderr on the `create` call. See specs/ralph.md 'Main Loop' and crates/ralph/src/main.rs:111-166.
close_reason: fixed

title: Extract sandbox_name() helper in ralph
description: Extract a `sandbox_name()` helper function that computes `claude-<workspace_dir_basename>` from the current working directory. Both `ensure_sandbox()` and `configure_sandbox_network()` currently duplicate this logic (main.rs:112-117 and 142-143). The helper should return a String. See specs/ralph.md 'Sandbox Pensa Configuration' for the naming convention.

title: Add sandbox_exists() check using docker sandbox ls -q
description: Add a `sandbox_exists(name: &str) -> bool` function that runs `docker sandbox ls -q` (via `docker_command()`), captures stdout, and checks if any line matches the given sandbox name. On command failure, log a warning and return false (fall through to create). See specs/ralph.md 'Main Loop' and 'Error Handling' table.

title: Update ensure_sandbox() to check before create
description: Update `ensure_sandbox()` to call `sandbox_exists()` before `docker sandbox create`. If the sandbox already exists, log at info level and return early. If it does not exist, call `create` with stdout/stderr set to `Stdio::null()`. Update `configure_sandbox_network()` to use the shared `sandbox_name()` helper. See specs/ralph.md 'Main Loop' for the updated flow.

title: Update ralph spec documentation
description: Update specs/ralph.md to reflect the changes. Already done during spec phase — verify the Main Loop section and Error Handling table are accurate after implementation. No other docs to update.

title: Integration test: no sandbox 'already exists' output leak
description: Add an integration test that verifies ralph does not print 'already exists' to stdout/stderr when launched against an existing sandbox. Use `--command` override to avoid requiring Docker. Test the `sandbox_exists()` and `sandbox_name()` functions directly with unit tests. Verify: (1) sandbox_name() returns `claude-<basename>`, (2) sandbox_exists() returns false when name not in ls output, (3) ensure_sandbox skips create when sandbox exists.

title: AFK double Ctrl+C broken: SIGINT leaks to ralph child process
description: sgf spawns ralph in the same process group, so terminal Ctrl+C (SIGINT) propagates to both sgf and ralph. This breaks sgf's double Ctrl+C logic in AFK mode — ralph receives the first SIGINT directly and dies before sgf can prompt for confirmation. Fix: use pre_exec with setsid() when spawning ralph in run_ralph() (crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs:206-216). See specs/springfield.md signal handling section.

title: Integration test: verify first Ctrl+C does not kill ralph in AFK mode
description: Update the existing integration test for AFK double Ctrl+C (crates/springfield/tests/integration.rs:1343) to verify that the first SIGINT does NOT kill ralph (ralph should still be running after the first signal). The test should confirm ralph only exits after the second SIGINT or after sgf sends SIGTERM.

title: fix: Use setsid() pre_exec when spawning ralph in AFK mode to isolate from terminal SIGINT

title: Integration test: verify sandbox template contains all mobile stack tools
description: End-to-end test: build the sandbox template (sgf template build), then verify all tools are present by running commands inside the container. Check: rustc, cargo, pnpm, yarn, node, playwright, gitleaks, madge, knip, type-coverage, pn. This can be a manual verification or an ignored integration test.
close_reason: Closed by user request

title: Remove docker-ctx crate from workspace
description: Delete crates/docker-ctx/ entirely. Remove it from workspace Cargo.toml members. Remove docker_ctx dependency from ralph and springfield Cargo.toml files. This is a leaf dependency — nothing else depends on it except ralph and springfield.
close_reason: Deleted crates/docker-ctx/ directory, removed docker-ctx dependency from ralph and springfield Cargo.toml, replaced all docker_command() calls with Command::new("docker") in ralph/src/main.rs, springfield/src/template.rs, and springfield/src/orchestrate.rs. All tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Remove Docker sandbox lifecycle from ralph
description: Remove all Docker sandbox code from crates/ralph/src/main.rs: sandbox_name(), configure_sandbox_network(), sandbox_exists(), ensure_sandbox(), stop_sandbox(), write_daemon_url(), remove_daemon_url(), DaemonUrlGuard. Remove the --template CLI flag. Remove create_pty_stdin() (PTY pair no longer needed without Docker). Remove all docker_command() calls. Remove docker_ctx dependency from Cargo.toml.
close_reason: Removed all Docker sandbox lifecycle from ralph and updated springfield orchestrate to stop passing --template. Agent now invoked directly via $AGENT_CMD.

title: Replace Docker invocation with direct AGENT_CMD invocation in ralph
description: Replace 'docker sandbox run claude -- <flags>' with '$AGENT_CMD <flags>' in both interactive and AFK modes. Add AGENT_CMD env var resolution at startup — fail with exit code 1 and clear error if unset/empty and --command is not set. When --command is set, skip AGENT_CMD check. Update banner to show agent binary path instead of sandbox template. Remove external file staging (stage_external_files) — no longer needed since agent runs on host with full filesystem access.
close_reason: Already implemented in prior commits (c03e868, abec4aa, cd2e3d1). All Docker invocation replaced with direct AGENT_CMD, resolve_agent_cmd() implemented, stage_external_files removed.

title: Remove Docker container detection from pensa client
description: Remove in_container() (/.dockerenv check), rewrite_localhost_in_container(), and the proxy-forcing logic in build_http_client() from crates/pensa/src/client.rs. The UrlSource::DaemonFile variant no longer needs special container handling. Simplify resolve_url() and build_http_client() accordingly. Keep PN_DAEMON_HOST and PN_DAEMON support as-is (still useful for remote daemon scenarios).
close_reason: Removed Docker container detection (in_container, rewrite_localhost_in_container, UrlSource enum, proxy-forcing logic). Simplified resolve_url() and Client::new().

title: Remove template system from springfield
description: Remove crates/springfield/src/template.rs entirely. Remove template::ensure_template() call from orchestrate.rs. Remove 'sgf template build' CLI subcommand from main CLI. Remove the .docker/ directory (Dockerfile). Remove the sha2 and tempfile (build) dependencies from springfield's Cargo.toml if only used by template. Remove docker_ctx dependency from springfield's Cargo.toml. Remove the include_str\! of the Dockerfile. Update CLAUDE.md to remove 'sgf template build' section.
close_reason: Removed template system: template.rs, Template CLI subcommand, .docker/ directory, ensure_template() preflight call. Cleaned up AGENTS.md and integration tests.

title: Update sgf orchestration to remove Docker dependencies
description: Update crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs: (1) Replace resolve_agent_cmd() to require AGENT_CMD env var — fail with clear error if unset/empty (no 'claude' fallback). (2) Remove stop_sandbox() function and all calls to it. (3) Remove template::ensure_template() call. (4) Remove --template from build_ralph_args(). (5) Ensure AGENT_CMD is passed through to ralph's environment. (6) Update run_interactive_claude to use the strict AGENT_CMD resolution. Update tests accordingly.
close_reason: resolve_agent_cmd() made strict (no fallback), AGENT_CMD validated before ralph launch, 3 new tests added. Docker items 2-4 were already removed.

title: Update ralph tests for direct agent invocation
description: Update crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs: tests already use RALPH_COMMAND which bypasses Docker, so most should continue to work. Verify all tests pass after the Docker removal. Add a test that verifies ralph exits 1 when AGENT_CMD is unset and --command is not set. Remove any test assertions about sandbox template names. Update sandbox_name_uses_cwd_basename unit test (remove it — no more sandbox names).
close_reason: Verified all 64 ralph tests pass. No sandbox/template assertions to remove. AGENT_CMD-unset test already exists. Updated README.md to remove Docker references.

title: Update springfield tests for Docker removal
description: Update crates/springfield/tests/integration.rs: remove any assertions about --template in ralph args. Update build_args tests to not expect --template flag. Verify all tests pass. Update crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs tests: remove build_args_no_sandbox_removed test (irrelevant), update build_args_build_afk_no_push to not expect --template.
close_reason: Removed stale Docker/sandbox assertions from springfield tests: dropped --template and --no-sandbox absence checks from integration.rs build_invokes_ralph_with_correct_flags, removed build_args_no_sandbox_removed unit test, and removed --template absence check from run_with_mock_ralph_exit_0.

title: Update documentation for Docker-to-native migration
description: Update CLAUDE.md: remove 'sgf template build' section, remove sandbox template building section. Update BACKPRESSURE.md: remove 'Sandbox note' about Docker sandbox and Maestro. Review .sgf/prompts/*.md for any Docker/sandbox references and update them. Verify specs/README.md is accurate.
close_reason: Updated all documentation for Docker-to-native migration. Removed Docker/sandbox references from BACKPRESSURE.md, README.md, crates/springfield/README.md, crates/pensa/README.md, and specs/springfield.md. Verified specs/README.md is accurate. All 210 tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.

title: Integration test: verify end-to-end workflow without Docker
description: End-to-end integration test that verifies the full workflow works without Docker: (1) AGENT_CMD is required and fails cleanly when missing, (2) ralph invokes the mock command with correct flags (--verbose, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --settings, --append-system-prompt, prompt), (3) sentinel file detection works on native filesystem (instant, no Mutagen delay), (4) auto-push works, (5) signal handling works without sandbox cleanup. Run with: cargo test -p ralph
close_reason: Added 6 integration tests covering agent invocation flag verification (AFK + interactive modes), inline text prompt arg format, and auto-push (enabled + disabled). All 47 integration tests pass.

title: Scaffold sandbox config in merge_claude_settings
description: Update merge_claude_settings() in crates/springfield/src/init.rs to scaffold the sandbox block in .claude/settings.json. Must merge additively: array fields (allowWrite, allowedDomains) append without duplicates; scalar fields (enabled, autoAllowBashIfSandboxed, allowUnsandboxedCommands, allowLocalBinding) set only if absent. See specs/springfield.md 'Claude settings' section for the full config shape. Update existing tests (settings_json_created_from_scratch, settings_json_merges_with_existing, settings_json_no_duplicates_on_rerun) and add new tests for sandbox-specific merge behavior.
close_reason: Scaffold sandbox config in merge_claude_settings with additive merge for arrays and set-if-absent for scalars

title: Add sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands override to ralph --settings
description: Update the --settings JSON in ralph's run_interactive() and run_afk() in crates/ralph/src/main.rs. Change from '{"autoMemoryEnabled": false}' to '{"autoMemoryEnabled": false, "sandbox": {"allowUnsandboxedCommands": false}}'. This prevents automated agents from escaping the sandbox. See specs/ralph.md agent invocation section.
close_reason: Added sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands override to ralph --settings in both interactive and AFK modes, and updated integration test.

title: Update springfield and ralph documentation for native sandbox
description: Review and update README.md to mention native sandbox support. Ensure BACKPRESSURE.md and other docs are consistent. Verify specs/springfield.md and specs/ralph.md changes from the spec phase are accurate after implementation.
close_reason: Updated README.md (root, ralph, springfield) to document native sandbox support. Specs were already accurate.

title: E2E test: verify sandbox config scaffolded by sgf init
description: Integration test that runs sgf init in a temp directory and verifies: (1) .claude/settings.json contains sandbox.enabled=true, autoAllowBashIfSandboxed=true, allowUnsandboxedCommands=true, filesystem.allowWrite includes ~/.cargo, network.allowedDomains includes localhost/github.com/crates.io, network.allowLocalBinding=true. (2) Re-running sgf init does not duplicate sandbox entries. (3) Existing sandbox config is preserved during merge (custom allowedDomains not lost). Run with: cargo test -p springfield
close_reason: Added 3 E2E integration tests verifying sandbox config scaffolding, idempotency, and merge preservation via the sgf binary

title: resolve_agent_cmd_set test is flaky due to env var race condition in parallel test execution
close_reason: Fixed in commit 7512cff: extracted pure function resolve_agent_cmd_from() so tests avoid env var mutation. All tests pass reliably.

title: fix: isolate resolve_agent_cmd_set test from env var race condition
close_reason: Refactored resolve_agent_cmd into a pure function resolve_agent_cmd_from(Option<String>); tests now call the pure function directly instead of mutating env vars, eliminating the race condition.

title: Add --no-push flag to sgf spec CLI command
description: In main.rs, change Commands::Spec from a unit variant to a struct variant with an optional --no-push flag. Pass this flag through LoopOpts to run_loop. See how Commands::Build handles LoopOpts for reference.
close_reason: Changed Commands::Spec from unit variant to struct variant with --no-push flag. Flag is passed through LoopOpts to run_loop.

title: Add auto-push after interactive Claude session for sgf spec
description: In orchestrate.rs run(), when config.interactive && config.stage == "spec" && \!config.no_push: capture git HEAD before run_interactive_claude(), compare after, and git push if changed. Add a git_head() helper (same as ralph's: git rev-parse HEAD). Push failures are non-fatal (eprintln warning). Only applies to the spec stage, not issues log.
close_reason: Added auto-push after interactive Claude session for sgf spec. Captures git HEAD before session, compares after, pushes if changed. Suppressed with --no-push. Push failures are non-fatal.

title: Update springfield spec documentation for sgf spec auto-push
description: Verify specs/springfield.md accurately reflects the implementation. Already updated during spec phase — this task is to confirm after implementation.
close_reason: Verified spec documentation matches implementation — no changes needed

title: E2E test: sgf spec auto-pushes after session with new commits
description: Integration test in crates/springfield that: (1) sets up a git repo with a bare remote, (2) uses a mock AGENT_CMD that creates a commit, (3) runs sgf spec orchestration with skip_preflight, (4) asserts git push happened (remote has the new commit). Also test --no-push suppresses push and that no push happens when HEAD is unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented 3 integration tests: spec_auto_pushes_after_session_with_new_commits, spec_no_push_suppresses_auto_push, spec_no_push_when_head_unchanged

title: Add style.rs module — ANSI escape code helpers (bold, dim, green, yellow, red) with NO_COLOR env var support. Pure functions, no dependencies. See specs/ralph.md 'ANSI Styling' section. Source: crates/ralph/src/style.rs (new file).
description: Implement bold(s), dim(s), green(s), yellow(s), red(s) functions that wrap strings in ANSI escape sequences. Check NO_COLOR env var at first call (lazy static or once_cell) to disable all styling. Unit tests for each function and NO_COLOR behavior.
close_reason: Implemented style.rs with bold, dim, green, yellow, red functions, OnceLock-based NO_COLOR support, and 10 unit tests. All pass clippy -D warnings.

title: Add banner.rs module — box-drawing banner renderer with render_box(title, lines). See specs/ralph.md 'Box Banner Formatting' section. Source: crates/ralph/src/banner.rs (new file).
description: Implement render_box(title, lines) -> String. Uses style.rs for dim borders and bold titles. Computes box width from longest content line (min 40 chars). Right-pads content lines with spaces. Supports title-only boxes (no body). Unit tests for alignment, min width, title-only, and long content.
close_reason: Implemented render_box(title, lines) in banner.rs with dim borders, bold title, min 40 width, right-padding, and title-only support. 6 unit tests covering alignment, min width, title-only, and long content.

title: Update format.rs — expand StreamEvent enum to handle user/system/result-with-usage events, return FormattedOutput enum instead of Option<String>. See specs/ralph.md 'Serde Types' and 'Public Formatting API' sections. Source: crates/ralph/src/format.rs.
description: 1. Add User { message: UserMessage }, System {}, and expand Result to include optional session_id and usage fields. 2. Add UserMessage, UserContentBlock (ToolResult with content + is_error), Usage structs. 3. Change format_line return type from Option<String> to FormattedOutput enum (Text, ToolCalls, ToolResults, Usage, Result, Skip). 4. Parse user events for tool_result content blocks, truncate to 15 lines. 5. Log Unknown and System events at debug level instead of silently dropping. 6. Update all existing unit tests for new return type. 7. Add new tests for user events, tool results, error results, truncation, system events, result-with-usage.
close_reason: Expanded StreamEvent enum with User, System, and Result-with-usage variants. Changed format_line to return FormattedOutput enum. Added FormattedToolCall struct, FormattedToolResult struct, UserMessage/UserContentBlock/Usage types. Caller applies ANSI styling. All 244 workspace tests pass.

title: Update TeeWriter to strip ANSI codes from log file output. See specs/ralph.md 'AFK Mode' section. Source: crates/ralph/src/main.rs (TeeWriter struct).
description: Add a strip_ansi(s) function that removes ANSI escape sequences using regex pattern \x1b\[[0-9;]*m. The TeeWriter::writeln and write_ansi_line methods write styled output to stdout but stripped output to the log file. Add regex dependency or implement with simple byte scanning (no regex crate needed for this pattern).
close_reason: Added strip_ansi function and integrated it into TeeWriter log file writes

title: Replace startup/iteration/completion banners with box-drawing style. See specs/ralph.md 'Startup Banner', 'Iteration Banner', 'Completion and Max-Iterations Banners' sections. Source: crates/ralph/src/main.rs (print_banner fn, iteration loop, completion/max-iterations output).
description: 1. Replace print_banner() to use render_box for the config block (keep Ralph ASCII art as-is above it). Title: 'Ralph Loop Starting'. Body: Mode, Prompt, Iterations, Agent, Loop ID, Prompt files. 2. Replace iteration ======== banners with render_box title-only boxes. 3. Replace completion banner with green-titled box. 4. Replace max-iterations banner with yellow-titled box. 5. Style 'Iteration N complete, continuing...' and 'New commits detected, pushing...' as dim.
close_reason: Replaced all ======== banners with box-drawing style: startup uses render_box with body lines, iteration/completion/max-iterations use title-only boxes with appropriate colors. Styled dim messages for iteration-complete and new-commits-pushing.

title: Update run_afk() to render styled output from FormattedOutput enum. See specs/ralph.md 'Tool Call Formatting', 'Tool Result Formatting', 'Text Block Formatting' sections. Source: crates/ralph/src/main.rs (run_afk fn).
description: Replace the current format_line() -> Option<String> consumption in run_afk() with the new FormattedOutput enum. Match on each variant and apply ANSI styling: 1. Text — print unstyled. 2. ToolCalls — print each as '  [dim]─[/dim] [bold]Name[/bold]  [dim]detail[/dim]'. 3. ToolResults — print each result indented 5 spaces, dim (or dim red for errors), with '... (N more lines)' for truncated. 4. Usage — print dim '  Input: N tokens · Output: N tokens'. 5. Result — print unstyled. 6. Skip — no output.
close_reason: Already implemented in commit 0ea533e. run_afk() correctly matches all FormattedOutput variants with spec-compliant ANSI styling. All tests pass.

title: Update NDJSON test fixtures to include user events with tool results. Source: crates/ralph/tests/fixtures/afk-session.ndjson.
description: Add user events with tool_result content blocks to afk-session.ndjson fixture, interleaved after the corresponding assistant tool_use events. Include: 1. A tool_result with multi-line text content (e.g., file contents from a Read). 2. A tool_result with short content (e.g., Edit success). 3. A tool_result with is_error: true (e.g., failed Bash command). 4. A tool_result with >15 lines to test truncation. If the real stream-json format does not emit user events, add a comment noting this and keep the fixture for unit test coverage.
close_reason: Updated afk-session.ndjson with 10 user events containing tool_result content blocks covering all required cases: multi-line text, short content, is_error:true, and >15 lines for truncation. All format unit tests and related integration tests pass.

title: Update integration tests for new AFK output format. See specs/ralph.md 'Integration Tests' section. Source: crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs.
description: Update existing integration tests and add new ones: 1. Update text assertions from '-> Read(...)' to '─ Read' format. 2. Add test: startup banner contains box-drawing chars (╭─ Ralph Loop Starting). 3. Add test: iteration banner contains box-drawing chars. 4. Add test: completion banner contains '╭─ Ralph COMPLETE'. 5. Add test: max-iterations banner contains '╭─ Ralph reached max iterations'. 6. Add test: NO_COLOR=1 produces no ANSI escape sequences. 7. Add test: tool results appear in output (if user events in fixture). All tests should set NO_COLOR=1 when asserting on text content to avoid ANSI codes in assertions.
close_reason: Added afk_no_color_disables_ansi and afk_shows_tool_results integration tests. All other tests from the description already existed.

title: Integration test no_loop_id_when_not_provided fails: expects old ======== border format but uncommitted main.rs changes use box-drawing banners. Test asserts stdout.contains("Iteration 1 of 1\n") which no longer matches the box format output.
close_reason: Bug already fixed in uncommitted working tree changes: main.rs uses render_box() for all banners, integration test assertions updated to match box-drawing format. All 100 tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.

title: Create crates/shutdown/ crate with ShutdownController — SIGINT counter, SIGTERM flag, stdin EOF monitor thread, poll() returning ShutdownStatus enum, separate Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D channels with 2-second timeout
description: Scaffold the new crate. Implement ShutdownConfig, ShutdownController::new(), poll() with Running/Pending/Shutdown states. Register SIGINT handler (signal-hook), SIGTERM handler, and optional stdin reader thread. Confirmation message printed to stderr on first press. Unit tests for SIGINT and SIGTERM paths.
close_reason: Implemented crates/shutdown/ with ShutdownConfig, ShutdownStatus enum, ShutdownController with SIGINT counting, SIGTERM flag, stdin EOF monitor thread, poll() with double-press timeout logic, and 6 unit tests. All pass.

title: Integrate shutdown crate into sgf — replace inline signal handling in orchestrate.rs with ShutdownController
description: Add shutdown dependency to crates/springfield/Cargo.toml. Replace sigint_count/terminated atomics and manual signal registration in orchestrate.rs with ShutdownController. All modes (AFK, non-AFK, interactive) use double-press via controller.poll(). Set SGF_MANAGED=1 in ralph's environment. Interactive stages: replace SIG_IGN with controller polling. setsid() for all child processes (not just AFK).
close_reason: Integrated ShutdownController into sgf orchestrate.rs — replaced inline signal handling, set SGF_MANAGED=1, setsid for all children, interactive mode uses controller polling instead of SIG_IGN.

title: Integrate shutdown crate into ralph — replace inline signal handling in main.rs with ShutdownController
description: Add shutdown dependency to crates/ralph/Cargo.toml. Replace sigint_count/interrupted atomics and manual signal registration in main.rs with ShutdownController. Check SGF_MANAGED env var to set monitor_stdin. All modes (AFK + interactive) use double-press via controller.poll(). Remove duplicated double Ctrl+C logic from run_afk(). Between-iteration sleep uses controller.poll().
close_reason: Integrated shutdown crate into ralph — replaced inline signal handling with ShutdownController, updated all modes and tests

title: Update existing sgf and ralph tests for new shutdown behavior
description: Update orchestrate.rs tests that reference signal handling. Update ralph integration tests (double_ctrl_c test assertions). Ensure all existing tests pass with the new shutdown controller. Non-AFK mode now uses double-press instead of single-press — update any tests that assumed single-press immediate exit.
close_reason: Added 4 sgf-level signal integration tests. All 282 workspace tests pass. nix added as dev-dependency for signal sending.

title: Integration tests: verify double Ctrl+C and Ctrl+D exit sgf build -a end-to-end
description: E2E tests using mock ralph script. Test: (1) double Ctrl+C exits 130, (2) single Ctrl+C resets after timeout, (3) SIGTERM exits immediately, (4) stderr contains 'Press Ctrl-C again to exit' on first press, (5) double Ctrl+D exits 130 with 'Press Ctrl-D again to exit' message, (6) mixed Ctrl+C then Ctrl+D does NOT trigger shutdown. Run from CLI: cargo test -p springfield -- --test integration
close_reason: Added double_ctrl_d_exits_130 and mixed_ctrl_c_then_ctrl_d_no_shutdown integration tests. Tests 1-4 already existed. All 6 tests now pass.

title: Add blue(), magenta(), cyan() color functions to style.rs with tests
description: Add three new ANSI color functions to crates/ralph/src/style.rs: blue (code 34), magenta (code 35), cyan (code 36). Add a tool_name_style(name) helper that returns bold+colored string based on tool category: Read/Glob/Grep→bold blue, Edit/Write→bold magenta, Bash→bold yellow, other→bold cyan. Add unit tests for each new function and for tool_name_style categories. Respect NO_COLOR.
close_reason: Added blue(), magenta(), cyan() color functions and tool_name_style() helper to style.rs. Integrated tool_name_style into tool call display. 22 style tests pass including category coverage.

title: Apply tool name colors and cyan details in AFK output rendering
description: Update crates/ralph/src/main.rs run_afk() tool call rendering (lines ~601-609): replace style::bold(&call.name) with style::tool_name_style(&call.name), replace style::dim(&call.detail) with style::cyan(&call.detail). This gives each tool category a distinct color and makes details cyan instead of dim gray.
close_reason: Replaced style::dim with style::cyan for tool call details in AFK output rendering. tool_name_style was already applied in a prior commit.

title: Bold agent text and improve error styling in AFK output
description: Update crates/ralph/src/main.rs run_afk(): (1) Wrap FormattedOutput::Text lines in style::bold() so agent reasoning stands out from tool output. (2) Change error tool result styling from dim(red(l)) to just red(l) — errors should not be dimmed.
close_reason: Wrapped FormattedOutput::Text lines in style::bold() and changed error tool result styling from dim(red(l)) to red(l)

title: Add test result highlighting (ok/FAILED) in tool result lines
description: Update crates/ralph/src/main.rs run_afk() tool result rendering (lines ~611-629): for non-error result lines, check if the line ends with ' ... ok' → style as dim(green(l)), or ends with ' ... FAILED' → style as red(l). All other non-error lines remain dim(l). This highlights test pass/fail in cargo test output.
close_reason: Added test result highlighting: lines ending with '... ok' render as dim green, lines ending with '... FAILED' render as red, in AFK tool result output.

title: Update AFK output integration tests for new color scheme
description: Update crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs: (1) Existing NO_COLOR tests should still pass (no ANSI codes in output). (2) Add a test without NO_COLOR that verifies tool call lines contain ANSI color codes (blue for Read, yellow for Bash, etc.). (3) Verify agent text lines contain bold ANSI codes. (4) Verify test result lines with ' ... ok' contain green ANSI codes. Run: cargo test -p ralph
close_reason: Added 3 new integration tests verifying ANSI color codes in AFK output: tool call colors (blue/yellow/magenta/cyan), agent text bold, and test result highlighting (green ok, red FAILED). Created test-results.ndjson fixture. All existing NO_COLOR tests still pass.

title: shutdown crate unit tests have signal cross-talk — tests fail when run in parallel due to process-global signal handlers
close_reason: Fixed signal cross-talk: store SigIds and unregister on Drop, mark signal tests #[serial] via serial_test crate

title: Fix: ralph save/restore terminal settings (tcgetattr/tcsetattr) around agent spawn to prevent agent from disabling ISIG via inherited stderr fd
description: The agent (Claude Code) modifies terminal settings via inherited stderr fd (e.g., tcsetattr to enable raw mode), disabling ISIG. This causes Ctrl+C to send byte 0x03 instead of SIGINT and Ctrl+D to send 0x04 instead of EOF. Fix: save terminal settings before agent spawn, restore after exit. Affects both AFK and interactive modes. See specs/ralph.md Terminal Settings Preservation section.
close_reason: Implemented terminal settings save/restore via tcgetattr/tcsetattr around agent spawn in ralph main.rs

title: Fix: sgf gate monitor_stdin and setsid on config.afk in run_ralph
description: orchestrate.rs run_ralph() unconditionally uses setsid() and monitor_stdin: true. In non-AFK (interactive) mode this causes: (1) stdin monitor thread consumes user keystrokes meant for Claude, (2) setsid prevents child from receiving terminal signals and accessing controlling terminal. Fix: only use setsid() and monitor_stdin: true when config.afk is true. See specs/springfield.md and specs/shutdown.md.
close_reason: Gated monitor_stdin and setsid on config.afk in run() and run_ralph(). Added test for non-AFK mode.

title: Update sgf signal integration tests for mode-dependent shutdown behavior
description: Existing signal tests send SIGINT directly by PID, bypassing terminal driver. Tests should verify: (1) AFK mode uses setsid and monitor_stdin, (2) non-AFK mode does NOT use setsid or monitor_stdin, (3) terminal settings are preserved across agent runs. Add test that verifies stdin passthrough in non-AFK mode (mock agent reads stdin and echoes to stdout).
close_reason: Added 7 integration tests verifying mode-dependent shutdown behavior: setsid in AFK vs inherited session in non-AFK, monitor_stdin gating, stdin passthrough, and flag/log-file correctness.

title: Add ralph integration test for terminal settings preservation
description: Test that ralph saves and restores terminal settings around agent spawn. Mock agent script that modifies terminal settings (e.g., stty raw), verify terminal is restored after ralph exits. Test both AFK and interactive modes.
close_reason: Added integration tests: interactive_restores_terminal_settings_after_agent_corrupts (PTY-based, verifies ISIG/ICANON restored after Python tcsetattr corruption) and afk_terminal_restore_graceful_with_non_tty_stdin (verifies no-op restore with piped stdin). Added helpers: open_pty, termios_check_script, termios_corrupt_script.

title: ralph integration tests afk_restores_terminal_settings_after_agent_corrupts and interactive_restores_terminal_settings_after_agent_corrupts fail with posix_openpt failed in sandboxed environments
close_reason: Made open_pty() return Option and test gracefully skips in sandboxed environments

title: AFK mode stdin isolation: agent inherits terminal fd and disables ISIG, preventing Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D from working
description: In AFK mode, sgf passes Stdio::inherit() for stdin to ralph, and ralph doesn't set stdin on the agent spawn. The agent (Claude Code) inherits the terminal fd and modifies terminal settings (disabling ISIG via tcsetattr). This causes Ctrl+C to emit byte 0x03 instead of SIGINT and Ctrl+D to emit byte 0x04 instead of EOF. Fix: pass Stdio::null() for stdin in AFK mode at both sgf→ralph and ralph→agent boundaries.
close_reason: Fixed by passing Stdio::null() for stdin in AFK mode at both sgf->ralph and ralph->agent boundaries. Updated specs/shutdown.md, specs/ralph.md, specs/springfield.md to document stdin isolation.

title: Flaky springfield integration tests: confirmation_message_on_first_ctrl_c, double_ctrl_c_exits_130, mixed_ctrl_c_then_ctrl_d_no_shutdown intermittently fail when run alongside other workspace tests
close_reason: Added SGF_READY_FILE readiness mechanism replacing fixed 500ms sleep in all 8 signal-handling integration tests

title: Change AFK assistant text styling from bold to bold+cyan in run_afk() — update style::bold(l) to style::cyan(&style::bold(l)) for Text and Result variants
description: In main.rs run_afk(), change FormattedOutput::Text rendering from style::bold(l) to style::cyan(&style::bold(l)). Also update FormattedOutput::Result to use the same cyan styling. Lookup: crates/ralph/src/main.rs lines 608-612 and 655-659.
close_reason: Updated FormattedOutput::Text and FormattedOutput::Result in run_afk() to use style::cyan(&style::bold(l)) instead of plain bold. Updated integration test to verify cyan+bold ANSI codes.

title: Hide tool results in AFK output — drop ToolResults variant in run_afk() match arm
description: In main.rs run_afk(), replace the FormattedOutput::ToolResults match arm (lines 623-645) with an empty block (like Skip). Tool results are still parsed by format.rs for the log file, but not rendered to terminal. Lookup: crates/ralph/src/main.rs lines 623-645.
close_reason: Replaced ToolResults match arm with empty block in run_afk(). Updated integration tests to verify tool results are hidden.

title: Update existing integration tests to reflect AFK output changes — tool results hidden, text is cyan
description: Update tests/integration.rs: (1) any test asserting tool result content appears in stdout should assert it does NOT appear, (2) if NO_COLOR tests assert on bold-only output, verify they still pass since bold+cyan with NO_COLOR produces plain text. Lookup: crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs.
close_reason: All test updates were already completed in implementation commits e4bf13a and 153bcfe. Verified all 57 integration tests pass — no additional changes needed.

title: Create vcs-utils crate with Cargo.toml (no deps), add to workspace members
description: Create crates/vcs-utils/ with Cargo.toml (name = "vcs-utils", no external dependencies, tempfile as dev-dep). Add "crates/vcs-utils" to workspace members in root Cargo.toml. Create src/lib.rs with git_head() and auto_push_if_changed() as specified in specs/vcs-utils.md.
close_reason: Created vcs-utils crate with git_head() and auto_push_if_changed() per spec. 4 unit tests + 1 integration test, all passing. No external dependencies.

title: Wire ralph to use vcs_utils::git_head and vcs_utils::auto_push_if_changed
description: Add vcs-utils = { path = "../vcs-utils" } to ralph/Cargo.toml. Remove the local git_head() and auto_push_if_changed() functions from main.rs. Replace all call sites with vcs_utils::git_head() and vcs_utils::auto_push_if_changed(before, |msg| tee.writeln(&style::dim(msg))). The auto_push enable check (cli.auto_push) stays in ralph.
close_reason: Replaced local git_head() and auto_push_if_changed() in ralph with vcs_utils crate. The cli.auto_push guard stays in ralph's wrapper.

title: Wire springfield to use vcs_utils::git_head and vcs_utils::auto_push_if_changed
description: Add vcs-utils = { path = "../vcs-utils" } to springfield/Cargo.toml. Remove the local git_head() and auto_push_if_changed() functions from orchestrate.rs. Replace call sites with vcs_utils::git_head() and vcs_utils::auto_push_if_changed(before, |msg| eprintln\!("sgf: {msg}")).
close_reason: Wired springfield to use vcs_utils::git_head and vcs_utils::auto_push_if_changed. Removed local duplicates from orchestrate.rs. Updated integration test to match vcs_utils message format.

title: Update ralph and springfield specs to document vcs-utils dependency
description: Update ralph spec: replace git_head/auto_push_if_changed docs with a reference to vcs-utils spec. Add vcs-utils to ralph's dependency table. Update springfield spec: note that auto-push uses vcs_utils::auto_push_if_changed.
close_reason: Updated ralph spec: added vcs-utils to dependency table, replaced inline git_head/auto_push docs with vcs-utils references. Updated springfield spec: noted auto-push uses vcs_utils::auto_push_if_changed.

title: Integration test: vcs-utils auto_push_if_changed pushes to local bare remote
description: In crates/vcs-utils/tests/integration.rs: create a temp dir with a bare git remote and a cloned working repo. Make a commit, call auto_push_if_changed with the old HEAD, verify the commit appears on the bare remote. Also test: unchanged HEAD emits nothing, git_head returns Some in a repo, git_head returns None outside a repo.
close_reason: Added 3 integration tests: unchanged_head_emits_nothing, git_head_returns_some_in_repo, git_head_returns_none_outside_repo. Used CWD_LOCK mutex for test isolation.

title: Add doc.md template to init.rs — embed with include_str\!, add to TEMPLATE_FILES array
description: Add TEMPLATE_DOC const and TemplateFile entry in init.rs so sgf init scaffolds .sgf/prompts/doc.md. Pattern: identical to TEMPLATE_ISSUES.
close_reason: Added TEMPLATE_DOC const with include_str\! and TemplateFile entry for .sgf/prompts/doc.md in init.rs

title: Add Doc command variant to CLI and wire to run_loop in main.rs
description: Add Commands::Doc { no_push: bool } to the enum in main.rs. Wire it in main() match — same pattern as Commands::Spec: construct LoopOpts with afk=false, iterations=1, call run_loop("doc", None, &opts, None, true).
close_reason: Added Commands::Doc { no_push: bool } variant to CLI enum and wired it in main() match arm — same pattern as Spec: LoopOpts with afk=false, iterations=1, run_loop("doc", ..., true)

title: Enable auto-push for sgf doc in orchestrate.rs
description: In orchestrate::run(), the auto-push guard currently checks config.stage == "spec". Change the condition to also trigger for "doc": config.stage == "spec" || config.stage == "doc".
close_reason: Extended auto-push condition in orchestrate.rs to trigger for doc stage alongside spec. Added integration test.

title: Update springfield spec documentation for sgf doc
description: Verify the spec edits made during planning are complete and accurate. Update any remaining references (README, etc.).
close_reason: Spec documentation verified complete. Fixed CLI help text for Doc command to match spec.

title: Integration test: sgf doc launches interactive session with doc.md prompt
description: Add a test in orchestrate.rs tests that verifies: (1) run() with stage="doc", interactive=true uses the .sgf/prompts/doc.md template, (2) auto-push fires when no_push=false (mock git_head), (3) auto-push is suppressed when no_push=true. Follow the existing run_with_mock_ralph_* test patterns but use a mock AGENT_CMD.
close_reason: Added doc_runs_interactive_via_agent_cmd, doc_no_push_suppresses_auto_push, and doc_no_push_when_head_unchanged integration tests in tests/integration.rs

title: Add style.rs module to springfield crate with ANSI primitives (bold, dim, green, yellow, red, white, no_color, strip_ansi), inverse badge rendering, and semantic output functions (action, success, warning, error, detail). Include unit tests for all functions including NO_COLOR fallback.
description: Create crates/springfield/src/style.rs mirroring ralph's style.rs structure for ANSI primitives but adding sgf-specific badge() and semantic output functions. Badge renders as bold inverse ' sgf ' (\x1b[1;7m sgf \x1b[0m). Falls back to 'sgf:' when NO_COLOR is set. Semantic functions: action(msg) badge+white, success(msg) badge+green, warning(msg) badge+yellow, error(msg) badge+red, detail(msg) indented dim no badge. All output to stderr. Add mod style to lib.rs.
close_reason: Added style.rs module with ANSI primitives (bold, dim, green, yellow, red, white, no_color, strip_ansi), inverse badge rendering, and semantic output functions (action, success, warning, error, detail). 26 unit tests covering colored/no-color modes, alignment, and strip_ansi.

title: Replace all eprintln\!/println\! calls in orchestrate.rs with styled output
description: Replace output in crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs: export_pensa() success/error/warning messages, interactive session launch (action), ralph launch (action) with detail line (stage/iterations/mode), exit_message calls (success/error/warning by exit code), auto-push callback (action for pushing, warning for push failed). See Console Output spec section for full message catalog.
close_reason: Replaced all eprintln\!/println\! calls in orchestrate.rs with styled output using style::print_action/success/warning/error/detail. Added detail lines for ralph launch and interactive session launch.

title: Replace all eprintln\! calls in recovery.rs with styled output
description: Replace output in crates/springfield/src/recovery.rs: 'recovering from stale state...' (action), 'recovery complete' (success), pn doctor warnings (warning), 'starting pensa daemon on port N...' (action), 'pensa daemon ready' (success). See Console Output spec section for full message catalog.
close_reason: Replaced all 6 eprintln\! calls in recovery.rs with styled output: recovering/daemon start → print_action, recovery complete/daemon ready → print_success, pn doctor warnings → print_warning

title: Replace all eprintln\!/println\! calls in main.rs and init.rs with styled output
description: Replace output in crates/springfield/src/main.rs: stage errors (error), init errors (error), status placeholder. Replace output in crates/springfield/src/init.rs: 'project scaffolded successfully' (success). See Console Output spec section for full message catalog.
close_reason: Replaced all eprintln\!/println\! calls in main.rs (stage errors, init error, logs error, status placeholder) and init.rs (overwrite warning, scaffolded success) with styled output

title: Integration tests for sgf styled console output
description: Add integration tests verifying styled output end-to-end. Tests should: (1) Run sgf with NO_COLOR=1 and verify badge falls back to 'sgf:' prefix with no ANSI codes. (2) Run sgf without NO_COLOR and verify output contains ANSI inverse badge sequence. (3) Verify exit messages use correct semantic styling (success for exit 0, error for exit 1, warning for exit 2). Use assert_cmd/predicates on sgf binary stderr. Test via mock ralph scripts with known exit codes.
close_reason: Added 6 integration tests verifying styled console output: NO_COLOR plain prefix fallback, ANSI inverse badge in colored mode, exit 0/1/2 semantic styling (green/red/yellow), and NO_COLOR exit message variants.

title: Replace badge() with badge_top/badge_mid/badge_bot in style.rs — emit 3-line rounded box (╭─────╮ / │ sgf │ / ╰─────╯) with dim borders and bold inverse label. Update DETAIL_INDENT to align with message text (8 chars). NO_COLOR fallback unchanged (sgf: prefix).
description: Ref: specs/springfield.md Console Output section. Look at: crates/springfield/src/style.rs (badge fn, styled_line fn, detail fn, DETAIL_INDENT constant). The badge_top/badge_mid/badge_bot fns replace the single badge() fn. styled_line becomes a 3-line emitter: top + mid+message + bot. detail text goes on the bot line instead of a separate indented line.
close_reason: Replaced badge() with badge_top/badge_mid/badge_bot. styled_line now emits 3-line rounded box. Added action_detail/print_action_detail for detail-on-bot-line. Updated DETAIL_INDENT to 8 chars. Updated orchestrate.rs callers. All 280 tests pass.

title: Update print_action/print_success/print_warning/print_error to emit 3-line box — each prints top+mid+bot to stderr. Update print_detail to append detail text on the bot line of the preceding box instead of standalone indented line.
description: Ref: specs/springfield.md Console Output section. Look at: crates/springfield/src/style.rs (print_action, print_success, print_warning, print_error, print_detail fns). Each print fn now emits 3 eprintln\! calls (top, mid+msg, bot). print_detail changes: instead of a standalone indented line, detail text appears to the right of ╰─────╯ on the bot line.
close_reason: Implemented 3-line box emission via separate eprintln calls in print_action/success/warning/error. Updated detail() to append text on bot line (╰─────╯ detail) instead of standalone indented line. Updated init.rs to use inline warning message.

title: Refactor callers that use print_detail immediately after a print_action/print_success — change to new API where detail is passed alongside the message so the bot line includes it.
description: Ref: specs/springfield.md Console Output section. Look at: crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs (ralph launch prints action then detail for stage/iterations/mode), crates/springfield/src/main.rs, crates/springfield/src/init.rs. The detail text must appear on the ╰─────╯ line, so callers that emit detail need to pass it in the same call rather than calling print_detail separately.
close_reason: Removed dead print_detail/detail. Added print_success_detail, print_warning_detail, print_error_detail and string-returning variants. All tests pass.

title: Update unit tests in style.rs for rounded-box badge — replace badge_colored/badge_no_color tests, update action/success/warning/error/detail test helpers to assert 3-line box output, verify strip_ansi works with box-drawing chars.
description: Ref: crates/springfield/src/style.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tests. All existing badge/styled-line tests need updating: badge_with_color helper becomes badge_top/mid/bot helpers, format_action etc produce 3-line strings, detail aligns to 8-char indent on bot line. Verify strip_ansi handles ╭╮╰╯│─ (these are plain Unicode, not ANSI, so should pass through unchanged).
close_reason: Added strip_ansi_preserves_box_drawing_chars test verifying ╭╮╰╯│─ pass through unchanged (both plain and ANSI-wrapped). Existing badge_top/mid/bot and styled_line tests were already updated for 3-line box output in prior commits.

title: Update integration tests for rounded-box badge — update no_color_badge_falls_back_to_plain_prefix and colored_output_contains_ansi_inverse_badge to verify 3-line box structure in stderr output.
description: Ref: crates/springfield/tests/integration.rs (no_color_badge_falls_back_to_plain_prefix, colored_output_contains_ansi_inverse_badge tests). Colored test should verify stderr contains ╭─────╮ and ╰─────╯ box borders plus the bold inverse sgf label on the middle line. NO_COLOR test should verify sgf: prefix is unchanged.
close_reason: Updated both integration tests: no_color test now also verifies absence of box-drawing borders; colored test now verifies full 3-line box structure (╭─────╮ top, bold inverse sgf mid, ╰─────╯ bot)
